name: Totie Fields
bio: Totie Fields was an American comedian who became a national star in the 1960s through frequent appearances on The Ed Sullivan Show, The Tonight Show, and other variety programs. Her act centered on bold, self-deprecating humor about her weight, appearance, and married life, making her one of the most prominent female stand-ups of her era.

timeline.ev: First Ed Sullivan Show booking after Ed Sullivan sees her at the Copacabana
Releases debut comedy album Totie Fields Live
Diagnosed with breast cancer; continues performing
Voted Entertainer of the Year by the AGVA; dies of a pulmonary embolism

accolades: AGVA Female Comedy Star of the Year (1978)

bits.yt_title: Totie Fields "Put On A Happy Face" on The Ed Sullivan Show
